title: hieracium stelligerum subsp. simonkaianum
intro: Hieracium stelligerum subsp. simonkaianum, also known as Simonka's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium stelligerum subsp. simonkaianum include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
